title: 法國柔道選手盧卡斯與台灣楊勇緯的交流與訓練

summaryText:
這集影片記錄了法國柔道選手盧卡斯（Lucas）在台灣與奧運柔道選手楊勇緯見面、交流及訓練的過程。盧卡斯自2018年起就關注楊勇緯的比賽，對他非常崇拜。在一次酒後，盧卡斯被朋友激將，決定向楊勇緯發出挑戰。團隊得知盧卡斯對楊勇緯的敬佩後，決定安排他們在台灣見面。

盧卡斯在法國除了是高中老師，也是柔道教練。他對這次與楊勇緯的會面感到非常緊張和興奮。楊勇緯的出現讓盧卡斯非常開心，他稱讚楊勇緯謙遜且友善。兩人互相介紹了各自國家的美食，楊勇緯分享了家鄉的炒麵，盧卡斯則介紹了法國的馬鈴薯培根焗烤（Tartiflette）。盧卡斯還向楊勇緯請教了比賽前的儀式，並收到了楊勇緯的幸運符和奧運銀牌。盧卡斯對能親手觸摸到奧運獎牌感到非常感動，深知這背後付出的巨大努力。

隨後，他們前往台灣體育大學的柔道館進行訓練。楊勇緯帶領大家進行了熱身和基本動作練習，盧卡斯也分享了他在法國教學時的趣味熱身方式。在訓練的最後，盧卡斯與一位台灣柔道選手進行了一場練習賽。儘管盧卡斯自認狀態不佳，但比賽過程激烈，最終以平手收場。盧卡斯對這次與楊勇緯的交流和訓練感到非常難忘，認為柔道超越了語言障礙，帶來了深刻的連結和尊重。

segments.title: 盧卡斯對楊勇緯的崇拜與挑戰的緣起
segments.summary: 法國柔道選手盧卡斯透露，他對這次台灣之行的大部分行程都不知情，但唯一知道的是將會見到台灣奧運柔道選手楊勇緯。盧卡斯自2018年起就一直關注楊勇緯的比賽。他分享自己除了是高中老師，也是柔道教練。盧卡斯回憶起上次在法國時，朋友在凌晨時分激將他，讓他向楊勇緯發出挑戰，而他當時在微醺狀態下答應了。他還嘗試用中文說出自己的名字和挑戰宣言。

segments.title: 團隊促成盧卡斯與楊勇緯的會面
segments.summary: 盧卡斯的團隊得知他對楊勇緯的崇拜後，決定安排他們在台灣見面。他們透過Instagram主動聯繫楊勇緯，並在與其經紀人溝通後，成功安排在台中一家法式餐廳會面。盧卡斯在等待楊勇緯時顯得非常緊張和興奮。楊勇緯抵達時，盧卡斯對他謙遜友善的態度印象深刻，並為能見到偶像而感到無比開心。

segments.title: 初次交流與語言學習
segments.summary: 盧卡斯稱讚楊勇緯為人謙虛，且很高興能與他們見面。他們談到了楊勇緯在奧運會上的表現，盧卡斯提到楊勇緯差點就能贏得比賽。楊勇緯教盧卡斯法語的「謝謝」（Merci）和「帥哥」（Beau gosse），盧卡斯則嘗試用中文稱讚楊勇緯「很漂亮」，但被糾正「漂亮」是形容女生，應該用「帥」來形容男生。

segments.title: 品嚐台灣美食：炒麵
segments.summary: 盧卡斯和楊勇緯一起品嚐了楊勇緯家鄉后里的特色美食：炒麵。楊勇緯提到這道菜是台灣人早餐或午餐常吃的，他的父親也很喜歡。盧卡斯對台灣人早餐吃炒麵感到驚訝。他們玩了一個小遊戲，三碗炒麵中有兩碗超辣，一碗微辣，結果盧卡斯抽到了超辣的。盧卡斯開玩笑說，吃完這碗麵感覺充滿力量，可以直接去砍柴了，也難怪楊勇緯在比賽中能如此勇猛。

segments.title: 賽前儀式與幸運符
segments.summary: 兩人分享了各自的賽前儀式。盧卡斯習慣在比賽後吃草莓軟糖。楊勇緯則展示了他從日本朋友那裡得到的幸運符，上面刻有他的名字和「必勝」二字。盧卡斯對這個幸運符的意義感到非常感動，認為這代表了柔道的精神。

segments.title: 品嚐法國美食：馬鈴薯培根焗烤
segments.summary: 盧卡斯向楊勇緯介紹了他最喜歡的法國菜：馬鈴薯培根焗烤（Tartiflette），這道菜由他們的廚師Benjamin準備。菜餚包含馬鈴薯、培根、洋蔥、白酒奶油醬和雷布羅雄起司。楊勇緯品嚐後非常喜歡，甚至還打包了一份帶走，讓盧卡斯感到非常開心和滿足。

segments.title: 觸摸奧運銀牌的感動時刻
segments.summary: 盧卡斯向楊勇緯提出想看他的奧運銀牌。當楊勇緯拿出獎牌時，盧卡斯表現得極度激動和感動，他深知這枚獎牌背後所付出的無數努力、傷痛和時間。他強調奧運獎牌的稀有性和重要性，並表示能親手觸摸到它是一次非常難得的機會。盧卡斯被這份榮耀深深觸動，甚至感動到快要哭出來。

segments.title: 柔道訓練：熱身與基本動作
segments.summary: 午餐後，盧卡斯一行人立即前往台灣體育大學的柔道館進行訓練。他們迅速換上柔道服，盧卡斯表示穿上柔道服站在道館裡，感覺又是一種不同的感動。楊勇緯首先帶領大家進行了日常的熱身動作，盧卡斯從中觀察到楊勇緯動作中的細節，理解了他為何如此出色。隨後，盧卡斯也示範了他在法國教學時會帶領學生做的趣味熱身，例如跳馬背和蛇形爬行，這些動作雖然看似幼稚，但能有效訓練心肺耐力。他們還練習了利用布條進行的握力訓練，盧卡斯認為這種訓練方式非常適合柔道選手。

segments.title: 練習賽（Randori）與深刻體會
segments.summary: 訓練結束後，他們進行了期待已久的練習賽。由於楊勇緯和盧卡斯體重差異較大，一位體重相近且有十年柔道經驗的台灣選手與盧卡斯對練。比賽開始後，盧卡斯發現對手非常強壯且是左撇子，這增加了比賽的難度。盧卡斯在30秒後率先取得半勝，但對手也很快追平。在比賽的最後20秒，盧卡斯已經精疲力盡，開玩笑說自己不該吃那麼多炸雞。最終比賽以平手收場。盧卡斯對對手的表現印象深刻，並再次體會到柔道運動的艱辛與挑戰。

segments.title: 柔道的精神與難忘的體驗
segments.summary: 盧卡斯向楊勇緯表達了誠摯的感謝，感謝他熱情的款待和這次難得的交流機會。他強調，柔道是一項非常豐富的運動，它超越了語言的隔閡，讓不同國家的人們能夠透過肢體和技術相互理解與尊重。每一次的比賽和訓練都是一次寶貴的經驗，盧卡斯表示這次與楊勇緯的交流將是他永生難忘的時刻。

segments.title: 下集預告：台灣粉絲的熱情款待
segments.summary: 在與楊勇緯的影片拍攝結束後，盧卡斯一行人離開台中前往台南，入住了一間很棒的Airbnb。他們驚喜地發現有粉絲為他們送來晚餐。盧卡斯對台灣粉絲的熱情和貼心感到非常感動，並品嚐了各種美味的台灣當地美食，包括雞湯、滷肉飯和甜點。他稱讚台灣的食物非常美味，並開玩笑說這是朋友阿毛唯一的好主意。下集將繼續記錄他們在台南的精彩旅程和與粉絲的互動。

translations.en:
# Judo Exchange: French Judoka Lucas's Encounter and Training with Taiwan's Yang Yung Wei

This episode documents the journey of French judoka Lucas as he meets, interacts, and trains with Taiwanese Olympic judoka Yang Yung Wei. Lucas has been following Yang Yung Wei's matches since 2018 and holds him in high regard. After a few drinks, Lucas was challenged by a friend to invite Yang Yung Wei to a match, and he agreed.

Lucas, who is both a high school teacher and a judo instructor in France, was visibly nervous and excited about meeting Yang Yung Wei. Yang Yung Wei's arrival brought immense joy to Lucas, who praised him for being humble and friendly. The two exchanged greetings and discussed Yang Yung Wei's Olympic performance, with Lucas noting how close he came to winning a medal. They also shared insights into their respective national cuisines: Yang Yung Wei introduced his hometown's fried noodles, while Lucas presented the French Tartiflette. Lucas learned about Yang Yung Wei's pre-match rituals and was deeply moved when he received Yang Yung Wei's lucky charm and Olympic silver medal, recognizing the immense effort behind such an achievement.

Following their meal, they headed to the judo hall at the National Taiwan University of Sport for a training session. After quickly changing into their judogis, Yang Yung Wei led the warm-ups and demonstrated fundamental judo movements. Lucas also shared some fun warm-up exercises he uses in his classes in France. The training culminated in a randori (practice match) between Lucas and another Taiwanese judoka. Despite Lucas admitting he wasn't in peak condition, the match was intense and ended in a draw. Lucas was deeply impressed by his opponent and reiterated that judo transcends language barriers, fostering profound connection and mutual respect.

## Segments:

### Lucas's Admiration for Yang Yung Wei and the Origin of the Challenge
*Time Range: 00:00 - 01:05*
French judoka Lucas reveals that he was largely unaware of the full itinerary for his trip to Taiwan, except for the exciting prospect of meeting Taiwanese Olympic judoka Yang Yung Wei. Lucas has been a keen follower of Yang Yung Wei's matches since 2018. He shares that, in addition to being a high school teacher, he is also a judo instructor. Lucas recounts a past incident in France where, after a few drinks, a friend dared him to challenge Yang Yung Wei, a challenge he accepted. He even attempts to introduce himself and his challenge in Chinese.

### The Team Facilitates Lucas's Meeting with Yang Yung Wei
*Time Range: 01:06 - 02:10*
Upon learning of Lucas's admiration for Yang Yung Wei, his team decided to arrange a meeting in Taiwan. They reached out to Yang Yung Wei on Instagram, and after coordinating with his agent, a meeting was set at a French restaurant in Taichung. Lucas was visibly nervous and excited while waiting for Yang Yung Wei. When Yang Yung Wei arrived, Lucas was deeply impressed by his humble and friendly demeanor, overjoyed to finally meet his idol.

### First Impressions and Language Exchange
*Time Range: 02:11 - 03:00*
Lucas praised Yang Yung Wei for his humility and genuine happiness to be there. They discussed Yang Yung Wei's Olympic performance, with Lucas noting how close he came to winning a medal. Yang Yung Wei taught Lucas the French words for "thank you" (Merci) and "handsome" (Beau gosse). Lucas, in turn, attempted to compliment Yang Yung Wei in Chinese with "Ni hen piao liang" (You are very pretty), but was corrected that "piao liang" is for girls, and "shuai" (handsome) is the appropriate term for boys.

### Tasting Taiwanese Cuisine: Fried Noodles
*Time Range: 03:01 - 04:19*
Lucas and Yang Yung Wei sampled Yang Yung Wei's favorite local dish from his hometown, Houli: fried noodles (chao mian). Yang Yung Wei explained that this dish is commonly eaten for breakfast or lunch in Taiwan, and his father particularly enjoys it. Lucas expressed surprise that it's a breakfast item. They played a game with three bowls of noodles, two super spicy and one mildly spicy, and Lucas ended up with a super spicy one. Lucas jokingly remarked that eating it made him feel powerful, ready for judo, and understood why Yang Yung Wei is so formidable in matches.

### Pre-Game Rituals and Lucky Charms
*Time Range: 04:20 - 05:30*
The two judokas shared their pre-game rituals. Lucas always carries strawberry candies in his bag to eat after a match. Yang Yung Wei showed his lucky charm (omamori), a wooden tag given by a Japanese friend, inscribed with his name and the words "Must Win." Lucas was deeply moved by the significance of the charm, seeing it as an embodiment of the spirit of judo.

### Tasting French Cuisine: Tartiflette
*Time Range: 05:31 - 06:25*
Lucas introduced his favorite French dish, Tartiflette, prepared by their chef Benjamin. The dish featured potatoes, bacon, onions, white wine cream, and Reblochon cheese. Yang Yung Wei thoroughly enjoyed it, even taking some leftovers, which delighted Lucas.

### The Emotional Moment of Touching an Olympic Silver Medal
*Time Range: 06:26 - 08:04*
Lucas asked to see Yang Yung Wei's Olympic silver medal. When Yang Yung Wei presented it, Lucas became incredibly emotional and touched, deeply understanding the immense effort, pain, and time invested in achieving such a feat. He emphasized the rarity and significance of an Olympic medal, expressing that touching it was a truly special and overwhelming experience, almost bringing him to tears.

### Judo Training: Warm-ups and Basic Drills
*Time Range: 08:05 - 11:08*
After lunch, Lucas and his team immediately headed to the judo hall at the National Taiwan University of Sport for a training session. They quickly changed into their judogis, and Lucas expressed a profound sense of connection upon stepping onto the mat. Yang Yung Wei led the warm-ups, demonstrating standard judo movements. Lucas observed the subtle details in Yang Yung Wei's technique, which revealed the secrets to his excellence. Lucas then introduced some fun French warm-up exercises he uses in his classes, such as leapfrog and

which, despite seeming childish, are great for cardio and mobility. They also practiced grip strength using fabric strips similar to judogi material, a method Lucas found particularly effective for judo training.

### Randori (Practice Match) and Profound Realizations
*Time Range: 11:09 - 14:30*
Following the drills, they moved on to the highly anticipated randori (practice match). Due to a significant weight difference between Yang Yung Wei and Lucas, another experienced Taiwanese judoka (81kg, 10 years of experience) was brought in to spar with Lucas. The match began, and Lucas quickly realized his opponent was strong and left-handed, adding to the challenge. Lucas scored a half-point (wazari) within 30 seconds, but his opponent soon equalized. With only 20 seconds left, Lucas was utterly exhausted, jokingly attributing it to eating too much fried chicken. The match ended in a draw. Lucas was deeply impressed by his opponent's skill and once again appreciated the demanding nature of judo.

### The Spirit of Judo and an Unforgettable Experience
*Time Range: 14:31 - 15:31*
Lucas sincerely thanked Yang Yung Wei for his warm welcome and the invaluable opportunity for exchange. He emphasized that judo is a profoundly enriching sport that transcends language barriers, allowing individuals from different cultures to connect and show respect through physical interaction and technique. Every match and training session is a great experience, and Lucas declared that this encounter with Yang Yung Wei would be an unforgettable moment for him.

### Next Episode Teaser: The Warm Hospitality of Taiwanese Fans
*Time Range: 15:32 - 16:35*
After filming with Yang Yung Wei, Lucas and his team left Taichung for Tainan, where they checked into a fantastic Airbnb. They were pleasantly surprised when fans delivered dinner to them. Lucas was deeply touched by the fans' warmth and thoughtfulness, enjoying a variety of delicious Taiwanese local dishes, including chicken soup, braised pork rice, and desserts. He praised the amazing food and jokingly credited his friend Amao with the
for this segment. The next episode promises to continue their exciting journey in Tainan and their interactions with the local community.
